schools.school_name: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. School

level.label: Level
level.desc: Grade band served: elementary, k8, middle, high, alternative.

is_closure_candidate.label: Likely closure candidate
is_closure_candidate.desc: One of the 15 schools Willamette Week ranked as most likely to be closed (2026-03-18). PPS has not released its own shortlist — that is expected Nov 2026.

closure_rank.label: WW rank
closure_rank.desc: Willamette Week's published rank (1 = smallest), based solely on enrollment numbers. Not a PPS rank.

students_per_sqft.label: Students / sqft
students_per_sqft.desc: Building crowding: 2025-26 enrollment ÷ 2009 KPFF square footage. Lower = more underused space.

year_built.label: Year built
year_built.desc: Year the main building was constructed.

square_feet.label: Square feet
square_feet.desc: Building square footage (2009 inventory — may predate recent bond expansions).

construction_type_2009.label: Construction
construction_type_2009.desc: Structural type: URM (unreinforced masonry), LRCW (reinforced concrete wall), Wood, Concrete, Steel, Masonry.

is_urm_building.label: URM?
is_urm_building.desc: Unreinforced masonry — highest seismic risk category.

urm_retrofit_cost_usd.label: URM retrofit $
urm_retrofit_cost_usd.desc: Estimated cost to retrofit this URM building (only populated for URM schools).

seismic_retrofit_status.label: Seismic retrofit
seismic_retrofit_status.desc: 'full' = full modernization, 'targeted' = roof/partial retrofit done, 'planned_*' = 2025 bond scheduled, blank = none.

is_title_i.label: Title I?
is_title_i.desc: Receives federal Title I-A schoolwide funding (high-poverty designation).

pct_ela_prof_2425.label: % ELA prof 24-25
pct_ela_prof_2425.desc: Percent of students meeting/exceeding on Oregon ELA state test (all grades, all students).

pct_math_prof_2425.label: % Math prof 24-25
pct_math_prof_2425.desc: Percent of students meeting/exceeding on Oregon Math state test (all grades, all students).

crdc_chronic_absent_2020.label: Chronic absent (CRDC)
crdc_chronic_absent_2020.desc: Count of students chronically absent (missed ≥15 days). Based on 2020 COVID year — likely suppressed.

crdc_idea_2020.label: IDEA (SPED) students
crdc_idea_2020.desc: Students on IEPs under IDEA (special education).

frl_free_lunch.label: Free lunch (count)
frl_free_lunch.desc: Students eligible for free meals — raw count.

frl_reduced_lunch.label: Reduced lunch (count)
frl_reduced_lunch.desc: Students eligible for reduced-price meals — raw count.

pct_free_lunch.label: % Free lunch
pct_free_lunch.desc: Share of students on free meals (free ÷ 2022 enrollment) — proxy for poverty.

pct_frl.label: % FRL
pct_frl.desc: Share of students eligible for free OR reduced-price meals — standard poverty proxy.

pct_direct_cert.label: % Direct cert
pct_direct_cert.desc: Share directly certified for free meals via SNAP/TANF/foster — proxy for deep poverty.

pct_asian.label: % Asian
pct_asian.desc: Share of students identifying as Asian, 2025-26.

pct_black.label: % Black
pct_black.desc: Share of students identifying as Black/African American, 2025-26.

pct_hispanic.label: % Hispanic
pct_hispanic.desc: Share of students identifying as Hispanic/Latino, 2025-26.

pct_white.label: % White
pct_white.desc: Share of students identifying as White, 2025-26.

pct_multiracial.label: % Multiracial
pct_multiracial.desc: Share identifying as two or more races, 2025-26.

pct_bipoc.label: % BIPOC
pct_bipoc.desc: Share of students identifying as any race/ethnicity other than White (1 − % White), 2025-26.

avg_prof_2425.label: Avg proficiency 24-25
avg_prof_2425.desc: Average of % ELA + % Math meeting/exceeding (2024-25). Single number for cross-school performance comparison.

service_load.label: Service load (weighted)
service_load.desc: Cost-weighted composite: 1.0×% deep poverty + 2.5×% SPED + 1.5×% English learners. Weights approximate per-pupil cost ratios from weighted-student-funding formulas (Boston, Hawaii). A higher index means heavier per-student support load.

support_staff_per_100.label: Support staff per 100
support_staff_per_100.desc: Counselor + social worker + psychologist FTE, per 100 students (denominator: 2020-21 CRDC enrollment). Reflects 2020-21 staffing — base allocations make smaller schools score higher per-pupil.

pct_chronic_absent_2021.label: % chronic absent (21)
pct_chronic_absent_2021.desc: Share of students who missed ≥10% of enrolled days in the 2020-21 school year. Post-COVID but still pandemic-era; treat as floor on current rates. Clamped at 100% — a few alternative schools' raw CRDC counts exceed point-in-time enrollment due to mid-year mobility.

suspensions_per_100_2021.label: OSS per 100
suspensions_per_100_2021.desc: Out-of-school suspension instances per 100 students (2020-21). Counts events not students; one student can be suspended multiple times.

counselors_fte_2021.label: Counselor FTE
counselors_fte_2021.desc: Full-time-equivalent school counselors as reported to CRDC for 2020-21.

social_workers_fte_2021.label: Social worker FTE
social_workers_fte_2021.desc: Full-time-equivalent school social workers as reported to CRDC for 2020-21.

prof_residual.label: Proficiency residual
prof_residual.desc: Each school's avg proficiency minus what a linear fit on % BIPOC would predict. Positive = outperforms demographics; negative = underperforms.

affordable_units_within_1mi.label: Afford. units in catchment
affordable_units_within_1mi.desc: Total existing affordable housing units inside the school's PPS attendance area (or a 1-mile radius for schools without a published catchment).

pipeline_affordable_units_within_1mi.label: Pipeline afford. units
pipeline_affordable_units_within_1mi.desc: Affordable units in projects currently in development inside the school's PPS attendance area (2023–2027).

pipeline_family_units_within_1mi.label: Pipeline family units
pipeline_family_units_within_1mi.desc: 2+BR pipeline units inside the school's PPS attendance area — proxy for future families with kids.

n_pipeline_projects_within_1mi.label: Pipeline projects
n_pipeline_projects_within_1mi.desc: Number of affordable housing projects in development inside the school's PPS attendance area.

permits_units_within_1mi_since_2022.label: Permitted units (2022+)
permits_units_within_1mi_since_2022.desc: New residential units on building permits issued since 2022-01-01 inside the school's PPS attendance area — single-family, ADUs, and multifamily (all tenures). Schools without a published catchment fall back to a 1-mile radius.

n_permits_within_1mi_since_2022.label: Permits (2022+)
n_permits_within_1mi_since_2022.desc: Number of residential building permits issued since 2022-01-01 inside the school's PPS attendance area. Permits = approved to build; not all reach completion.

nearest_alt_school_mi.label: Miles to nearest alt.
nearest_alt_school_mi.desc: Great-circle distance from this closure candidate to the nearest non-candidate school of the same grade band (elementary, k8, middle, or alternative). A rough proxy for transportation impact: families would have to travel at least this far if their school closes (district reassignment may differ).

nearest_alt_school_name.label: Nearest alt. school
nearest_alt_school_name.desc: Name of the closest non-candidate school of the same grade band.

scatters.title: Enrollment vs. building crowding
scatters.subtitle: Closure candidates cluster in the bottom-left (small + underused buildings).

scatters.title: Math proficiency vs. % low-income
scatters.subtitle: Classic income-achievement gradient. Schools above the trend line outperform expectations; below, underperform.

scatters.title: Avg proficiency vs. deep poverty (direct certification)
scatters.subtitle: Strongest correlation in the dataset (r = -0.90). Direct certification (SNAP/TANF/foster) predicts test scores more tightly than free/reduced lunch or any race/ethnicity variable.

scatters.title: Enrollment change vs. nearby residential permits (2022+)
scatters.subtitle: Schools losing students while their neighborhood is actively being built warrant a closer look before closure.

scatters.title: Year built vs. enrollment
scatters.subtitle: Many candidates are mid-century buildings with low current enrollment.

scatters.title: Enrollment vs. % BIPOC students
scatters.subtitle: Do the 15 smallest-enrollment schools serve a disproportionately BIPOC student body compared with the rest of PPS?

scatters.title: URM retrofit cost vs. enrollment
scatters.subtitle: URM buildings only. The slope is cost-per-student to save; schools well above the trend are expensive to retrofit per retained seat.

scatters.title: Chronic absenteeism vs. avg proficiency (2020-21)
scatters.subtitle: Strong inverse relationship: schools where 1 in 3+ students were chronically absent post-COVID rarely exceed 30% proficiency now. Some candidates carry markedly higher chronic-absence rates than the district median.

scatters.title: Weighted service load vs. enrollment
scatters.subtitle: Cost-weighted index: 1.0×% deep poverty + 2.5×% SPED + 1.5×% English learners (weights from WSF formulas). Top-right schools absorb the most need; closing them concentrates services elsewhere.

0.name: High-need urban
0.desc: Mid-poverty, BIPOC-majority elementaries, mostly stable enrollment, lower test scores.

1.name: Large + growing
1.desc: Larger schools with strong nearby housing pipeline; no closure candidates.

3.name: Aging, high-SPED
3.desc: Tiny cluster: very old buildings, very high SPED %, declining enrollment.
